hardware.hapticProfile: Chaotic alternating pulses: cramping bursts, heavy pressure, then hollow silence.

hero.primaryPattern: IBS may involve gut-brain axis sensitivity, altered motility, visceral hypersensitivity, food triggers, stress, diarrhea, constipation, bloating, and pain.
hero.safetyPreview: Blood, black stool, weight loss, fever, anemia, nighttime diarrhea, persistent vomiting, severe/worsening pain, or new symptoms after age 50 needs medical evaluation.

biologyPathway.title: Gut-Brain Axis
biologyPathway.short: The brain and gut signal each other.
biologyPathway.detail: IBS involves communication between the brain, gut nerves, microbiome, immune signals, stress system, and digestive movement.

biologyPathway.title: Visceral Sensitivity
biologyPathway.short: The gut feels signals more intensely.
biologyPathway.detail: People with IBS may have heightened sensitivity to stretching, gas, stool movement, or normal digestive activity.

biologyPathway.title: Motility Changes
biologyPathway.short: Movement can speed up, slow down, or alternate.
biologyPathway.detail: IBS can involve diarrhea, constipation, mixed patterns, bloating, urgency, incomplete evacuation, and abdominal pain.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger Loop
biologyPathway.short: Stress, food, sleep, and hormones can interact.
biologyPathway.detail: Stress does not make IBS imaginary. It can amplify motility, sensitivity, pain, urgency, and the loop of symptoms causing more anxiety.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Disorder of Gut-Brain Interaction
body: IBS is medically a disorder of gut-brain interaction.
It commonly involves abdominal pain related to bowel habits, diarrhea, constipation, mixed patterns, bloating, urgency, and visceral sensitivity.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Motility and Sensitivity
body: The gut may move too quickly, too slowly, or unpredictably.
Nerves in the gut may also become more sensitive to gas, stretching, stool movement, or normal digestion.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Triggers and Amplifiers
body: Food triggers, stress, sleep, hormones, infection history, microbiome changes, and nervous-system arousal can all influence symptoms.
This does not mean IBS is fake. It means the gut and brain are stuck in a deeply annoying group chat.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Red Flag Check
body: Blood, black stool, unintentional weight loss, fever, anemia, nighttime diarrhea, persistent vomiting, severe or worsening pain, family history concerns, or new symptoms after age 50 require medical evaluation.
Do not label every bowel problem IBS just because the acronym is convenient.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Airport With a Screaming Control Tower
paragraphs: Your digestive system is an airport stuck in permanent weather delays.
The control tower, also known as your nervous system, cannot decide whether to ground all flights or clear every runway immediately.
GROUND ALL FLIGHTS becomes constipation. CLEAR EVERYTHING NOW becomes diarrhea. The baggage carousel is either spinning like a carnival ride or dead in the corner.
The passengers, your emotions and partially digested life events, are extremely upset.
There is a sign that says WE'RE DOING OUR BEST in Comic Sans. Someone wrote OUR BEST IS NOT GOOD ENOUGH underneath. Rude, but fair.
The goal is not to fire the airport. The goal is to stop running every Tuesday afternoon like an international incident.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Indigestible Life Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have an IBS problem. You may also have a processing-overload problem.
Do not stop at food blame. Food matters. FODMAPs may matter. Dairy may matter. Gluten may matter for some people. Medical evaluation matters.
But if your gut goes chaotic every time your life does, ask what your system cannot process.
What obligation are you swallowing? What relationship keeps turning your stomach? What pressure keeps flipping you between hold everything and evacuate immediately?
Your gut is not your enemy. It is an overreactive messenger with terrible timing.
Stabilize the basics: food tracking, sleep, stress rhythm, medical rule-outs, and actual boundaries.
Land one plane at a time or keep letting the control tower scream. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen-Liver Disharmony
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Spleen is associated with processing and the Liver with smooth flow.
Symbolically, IBS can mirror processing disrupted by worry, frustration, and stuck movement.

influenceLayers.title: Fractured Conveyor Archetype
paragraphs: The Flowing One moves through life without forcing hold or release.
When suppressed, the gut may alternate between too much control and too little containment.

influenceLayers.title: Gut as Weather System
paragraphs: IBS can symbolically feel like an internal climate that changes without warning.
The goal is not domination. It is pattern recognition.

influenceLayers.title: Indigestible Life
paragraphs: This symptom can ask what part of life feels impossible to digest.
The body may be reacting to both food and context.

influenceLayers.title: Control Tower Reset
paragraphs: The reset is rhythm: meals, sleep, breath, boundaries, and medical guidance.
One plane at a time. One signal at a time.

reset.title: Gut-Brain Regulation & Control Tower Reset
steps: Sit or lie down with both hands on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
On each exhale, whisper: I process what is mine. I release what is not. One signal at a time.
Continue for 2 minutes, then write one trigger, one food pattern, and one life stressor without turning it into a courtroom drama.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. IBS symptoms should be evaluated when red flags appear or symptoms are new, severe, or worsening.

items.label: Pattern tracking
items.detail: Track food, stress, sleep, bowel pattern, pain, bloating, and urgency.

items.label: Regular rhythm
items.detail: Consistent meals, sleep, hydration, and movement may support gut regulation.

items.label: Trigger experiments
items.detail: Diet changes should be careful and trackable, not chaotic pantry warfare.

items.label: Stress regulation
items.detail: Longer exhales, grounding, therapy, and boundaries may reduce gut-brain amplification.

avoid: Do not ignore blood, black stool, weight loss, fever, anemia, nighttime diarrhea, persistent vomiting, severe/worsening pain, or new symptoms after age 50.
Do not assume every gut symptom is IBS without medical rule-outs.

brutalActions: Track food and life stress together.
Stop blaming one random meal for a whole nervous-system pattern.
Create rhythm: sleep, meals, hydration, movement.
Get medical care for red flags or new/worsening symptoms.

clinical.mechanism: IBS is a disorder of gut-brain interaction involving abdominal pain with changes in bowel habits, visceral hypersensitivity, motility changes, stress sensitivity, food triggers, and altered gut signaling.
protocol: Track food, stress, sleep, bowel pattern, pain, bloating, and urgency.
Seek medical evaluation for red flags or new/worsening symptoms.
Use clinician-guided strategies for IBS subtype, diet, medications, stress, and bowel pattern.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical diagnosis or IBS care.

witty.metaphorTitle: The Airport With a Screaming Control Tower
witty.metaphorText: Your gut airport keeps switching between grounding every flight and launching everything at once.
witty.wittyAdvice: Calm the control tower before baggage claim becomes a crime scene.

brutal.realityCheck: You do have an IBS problem. You may also have a processing-overload problem.
brutal.protocolTitle: LAND ONE PLANE AT A TIME
protocolSteps: Use medical guidance instead of random food panic.

biologyPath.title: Subconscious Alarm
biologyPath.description: Your mind registers a threat—usually related to loss of control or fear of instability.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The amygdala activates a defense posture, diverting focus from internal homeostasis to external threat monitoring.

biologyPath.title: Vagal Shutdown
biologyPath.description: The vagus nerve drops its parasympathetic 'rest & digest' signaling to the GI tract.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Blood flow is shunted away from the abdominal organs to feed the limbs for survival, halting normal enzymatic digestion.

biologyPath.title: CRF Release
biologyPath.description: Stress hormones trigger local inflammation directly inside the intestinal lining.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Corticotropin-releasing factor activates mucosal mast cells, which release cytokines that irritate the enteric nervous system.

biologyPath.title: Smooth Muscle Spasm
biologyPath.description: Intestinal smooth muscles contract irregularly, leading to cramps and transit errors.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The rhythmic contractions of peristalsis are replaced by hyper-reactive spasming, either stalling transit completely or rushing it through.

biologyPath.title: Visceral Flare
biologyPath.description: Nerves in your gut become hyper-sensitive, translating normal gas into extreme pain.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Localized hypersensitivity may contribute to a feedback loop, telling your brain that your digestive tract is in a state of terminal collapse.

Unintentional weight loss, fever, or anemia symptoms
Nighttime diarrhea that wakes you from sleep
Persistent vomiting or dehydration
Severe, worsening, or localized abdominal pain
New bowel symptoms after age 50 or strong family history of colon cancer, IBD, or celiac disease
seek_immediate_care_if: You have severe pain, blood/black stool, persistent vomiting, dehydration, fainting, or serious illness signs
Symptoms are rapidly worsening or unlike your usual IBS pattern
You have fever, severe weakness, or signs of obstruction/infection
schedule_care_if: Symptoms are new, worsening, persistent, or affecting daily life

medical_safety.do_not_ignore: IBS should not be used as a catch-all label for unexplained gut symptoms with red flags. It is not purely emotional.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure disease.

hardware.hapticProfile: Hot, burning, ascending pulses that rise like acid climbing a pipe.

hero.primaryPattern: Lower esophageal sphincter function, reflux pressure, acid exposure, meal timing, and stress-related symptom sensitivity may amplify GERD patterns.
hero.safetyPreview: Chest pain, trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, weight loss, or persistent/worsening reflux needs medical evaluation.

biologyPathway.title: Pressure Builds
biologyPathway.detail: Large meals, lying down soon after eating, certain triggers, abdominal pressure, hiatal hernia, pregnancy, some medications, and stress-related tension can all increase reflux pressure.

biologyPathway.title: LES Barrier Weakens
biologyPathway.short: The reflux valve does not hold fully.
biologyPathway.detail: The lower esophageal sphincter normally helps keep stomach contents down. When the barrier is weak or overwhelmed by pressure, acid and stomach contents can move upward.

biologyPathway.title: Acid Exposure
biologyPathway.detail: The esophagus is not built to handle repeated acid exposure. This can create burning, sour taste, regurgitation, throat irritation, cough, or hoarseness.

biologyPathway.title: Symptom Loop
biologyPathway.short: Fear and focus can make the burn louder.
biologyPathway.detail: Stress does not mean reflux is imaginary. Stress can change sensitivity, breathing, tension, and perception of discomfort, making symptoms feel more intense or harder to ignore.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Reflux Pressure
body: Acid reflux happens when stomach contents move upward into the esophagus.
This can be influenced by meal size, timing, lying down after eating, abdominal pressure, trigger foods, hiatal hernia, pregnancy, smoking, weight changes, and some medications.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Lower Esophageal Sphincter Function
body: The lower esophageal sphincter is the valve-like barrier between the stomach and esophagus.
When it relaxes inappropriately, weakens, or is overwhelmed by pressure, reflux can occur.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Esophageal Irritation
body: Repeated acid exposure can irritate the esophagus and throat.
Symptoms may include heartburn, sour taste, regurgitation, cough, hoarseness, throat burning, nausea, or chest discomfort.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Stress and Symptom Sensitivity
body: Stress can amplify reflux symptoms by changing breathing, muscle tension, sleep, eating patterns, and nervous-system sensitivity.
That does not make reflux symbolic only. It means the body and stress system are rude enough to collaborate.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary and the Backwards Sewer System
paragraphs: Your digestive system is now a poorly designed, underfunded sewer system in a town that keeps flushing things it should not.

Your stomach is the tank. Normally, flow goes downward. But two furious supervisors named Cortisol and Adrenaline keep hitting the backflow button because you never know when you might need to revisit something.
The lower esophageal sphincter is the valve at the top of the tank. It is supposed to close tightly. One-way flow only. But Gary the pump operator has not had a break since 2004.
Gary keeps leaving the valve ajar. The pressure rises. The acid backs up into the esophagus, which is not designed for this nonsense.

The control room has a sign that says DON'T PANIC in Comic Sans. There is a coffee mug that says WORLD'S OKAYEST PUMP OPERATOR. Gary is doing his best, which is upsetting, because his best is currently acid in your throat.
And you keep taking antacids. Antacids can lower the burn. They do not necessarily fix the pressure, the timing, the valve behavior, or the emotional habit of swallowing fire and calling it dinner.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Swallowed Fire Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a reflux problem. You may also have an I will just keep it down problem.
How many times have you felt heat rise in your chest, anger in your throat, and truth behind your teeth, then swallowed it like a shot of poison?
Every it is fine that was absolutely not fine has to go somewhere. Every unfinished argument. Every swallowed insult. Every truth that scared you.
Food can trigger reflux. Spicy food, late meals, chocolate, alcohol, coffee, stress eating, and lying down too soon can matter. But sometimes the burn also asks what you keep refusing to say.
Medication can matter. Lifestyle changes can matter. Medical care can matter. But if your whole life is one long performance of swallowing fire, do not act shocked when the fire keeps rising.
The next time the burn shows up, ask: what am I keeping down that wants to come up?
Speak it safely. Write it. Say it to the mirror. Stop making your esophagus hold a press conference for every sentence you buried.
Armor down or stay inflamed. Charming little bargain.

influenceLayers.title: Stomach Qi Rebellion
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, Stomach Qi normally descends. Food, experience, and emotion are meant to move downward and onward.
Reflux symbolically mirrors a reversal: what should descend rises instead.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Fire Invading Stomach
paragraphs: The Liver is associated with smooth flow and anger. When anger is stuck or swallowed, the symbolic Liver pattern can press into the Stomach.
The result is fire moving upward: heat, pressure, irritation, and the feeling of words burning their way back up.

influenceLayers.title: Swallowed Fire Archetype
paragraphs: The Speaker is the part that names what is wrong.
When that voice is suppressed long enough, the throat and chest become the symbolic chimney for fire that was never spoken.

influenceLayers.title: Throat Gate
paragraphs: The symptom may ask what truth is trying to rise.
The body may be saying: this cannot stay down forever.

influenceLayers.title: Descending the Fire
paragraphs: The goal is downward settling: upright posture, slower exhale, less pressure, and honest naming.
Do not use emotional reflection as a substitute for reflux care, medication guidance, or evaluation of concerning symptoms.

reset.title: Stomach Qi Descending & Liver Fire Cooling
steps: Sit upright. Place one hand on the sternum and one hand on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, imagining cool silver light moving from throat to stomach.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining heat leaving through the hands.
On each exhale, whisper: I do not have to swallow what burns me. I release what I should have said.
Continue for 2 minutes. Stay upright afterward.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Persistent or concerning reflux symptoms need medical evaluation.

items.label: Meal timing
items.detail: Avoid lying down soon after eating; many people do better staying upright after meals.

items.label: Smaller meals
items.detail: Large meals can increase reflux pressure.

items.label: Trigger tracking
items.detail: Track foods, timing, alcohol, caffeine, stress, sleep, and symptoms.

items.label: Sleep position
items.detail: Some people use head-of-bed elevation or left-side sleeping for nighttime reflux support.

avoid: Do not ignore chest pain, trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, or unexplained weight loss.
Do not stop prescribed reflux medication without medical guidance.

brutalActions: Ask: what truth keeps rising?
Write the sentence you keep swallowing.
Stay upright and use slow longer exhales.
Get medical care for chest pain, trouble swallowing, bleeding signs, weight loss, or persistent symptoms.

clinical.mechanism: Acid reflux and GERD involve stomach contents moving upward into the esophagus, often related to lower esophageal sphincter function, pressure, meal timing, irritation, and symptom sensitivity.
protocol: Stay upright after meals and track triggers.
Consider smaller meals and avoid late heavy eating.
Seek medical care for chest pain, trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, weight loss, or persistent symptoms.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for reflux diagnosis or medical care.

witty.metaphorTitle: Gary and the Backwards Sewer System
witty.metaphorText: Your stomach is an underfunded sewer tank and Gary the exhausted pump operator keeps leaving the top valve open.
witty.wittyAdvice: Give Gary a break, lower the pressure, and stop feeding the backflow machine at midnight like a goblin with a death wish.

brutal.realityCheck: You do have a reflux problem. You may also have an I will just keep it down problem.
protocolSteps: Write the truth without making it pretty.
Stay upright and slow the exhale.
Get medical care for red flags or persistent reflux.

hero.safetyPreview: Severe, worsening, localized, persistent, feverish, bloody, pregnancy-related, or unexplained abdominal pain needs medical evaluation.

biologyPathway.title: Gut-Brain Signal
biologyPathway.short: Stress and emotion talk to the gut.
biologyPathway.detail: The gut is heavily connected to the nervous system. Stress, fear, anger, meals, hormones, and inflammation can all influence how strongly the digestive tract reacts.

biologyPathway.title: Enteric Nervous System Alert
biologyPathway.short: The gut's nerve network becomes reactive.
biologyPathway.detail: The enteric nervous system helps coordinate digestion. When it becomes sensitized or dysregulated, normal movement, gas, or stretching can feel painful.

biologyPathway.title: Motility Disruption
biologyPathway.short: Movement becomes too fast, too slow, or chaotic.
biologyPathway.detail: Cramping can occur when intestinal contractions become uncoordinated. This may come with bloating, gas, diarrhea, constipation, or the charming sensation that your intestines joined a marching band.

biologyPathway.title: Smooth Muscle Spasm
biologyPathway.detail: The intestinal wall contains smooth muscle. When contractions become excessive or poorly coordinated, the result can feel like twisting, knotting, squeezing, or sharp cramps.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Gut-Brain Axis Activation
body: Abdominal cramps can involve communication between the brain, autonomic nervous system, vagus nerve, immune system, and enteric nervous system.
Stress does not make the pain fake. It can change motility, muscle tension, sensitivity, and the way the gut interprets normal pressure or movement.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Enteric Nervous System Sensitivity
body: The enteric nervous system helps coordinate digestive movement. When it becomes reactive, normal digestive activity may feel exaggerated, painful, urgent, or chaotic.
This can show up with cramping, bloating, gas, diarrhea, constipation, or alternating patterns.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Smooth Muscle Spasm
body: The intestines use smooth muscle contractions to move contents forward. If contractions become irregular or excessive, the sensation can be twisting, squeezing, knotting, or sharp cramping.

Medically, this can overlap with many possibilities, from gas and constipation to infection, food intolerance, menstrual pain, IBS, inflammatory bowel disease, gallbladder issues, appendicitis, or other causes.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Mild familiar cramps are different from severe, worsening, localized, persistent, or unusual abdominal pain.

Fever, vomiting, blood in stool, black stool, fainting, pregnancy, severe tenderness, inability to pass stool or gas, or pain in the lower right abdomen should not be treated as a metaphorical scavenger hunt.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Airport Security Line From Hell
paragraphs: Your digestive system is now a chronically understaffed airport security line during the holiday rush. The TSA agents, also known as your enteric nervous system, have been working triple shifts without breaks.

Your intestines are the conveyor belt. Normally, luggage moves along, gets sorted, and exits appropriately. But stress hormones keep slapping the emergency stop button because something might be suspicious.
Food? Suspicious. Air? Suspicious. That thing you wanted to say to your boss three years ago? Highly suspicious.
The conveyor belt stutters, stops, jerks forward, and jams again. That is the cramp. Not exactly a luxury travel experience.
The baggage claim carousel is also broken. One suitcase has been circling for years. It is that argument you never finished. The owner is nowhere to be found because, tragically, the owner is you.

The lost-and-found has a sign that says EMOTIONAL BAGGAGE: PLEASE CLAIM WITHIN 30 DAYS. The sign was posted in 2004. The hallway is now a mountain of unclaimed luggage. The cramping is the mountain shifting.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Swallowed Truth Reality Check
paragraphs: You may not have only a gut problem. You may also have an I will just swallow this problem. A don't make a scene problem. A what is the point of saying anything problem.
How many times have you wanted to say stop and did not? How many times did your stomach clench while your mouth smiled and nodded like a badly trained customer service employee?
Every swallowed insult, protest, boundary, truth, need, and no has to go somewhere. If it never leaves your mouth, your body may start storing it lower down.
Food may be the trigger. That does not mean food is always the whole story. Sometimes food is just the messenger unlucky enough to walk into a room full of old emotional explosives.
The next time your stomach tightens, ask the rude but useful question: what am I not saying?
Say it safely. Write it. Speak it to the mirror. Tell the truth somewhere your body can hear you. Your gut was not built to be a vault for every sentence you were too tired or scared to finish.
Armor down or stay twisted. Annoying little choice, but there it is.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Transportation & Transformation
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Spleen is associated with transforming food, experience, and thought into usable energy.
When worry and overthinking dominate, the symbolic Spleen pattern can feel bogged down: too much rumination, not enough movement.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Qi Stagnation
paragraphs: The Liver is associated with the smooth flow of Qi. Frustration, anger, and suppressed action are classic symbolic themes here.
When the Liver pattern feels stuck, the gut may become the place where blocked movement is felt as twisting, pressure, or cramping.

influenceLayers.title: Swallowed Truth Archetype
paragraphs: The Expresser is the part that knows how to say this is wrong, I am angry, I need this, and no.
When that voice is suppressed long enough, the gut becomes the prison for what the mouth would not release.

influenceLayers.title: Digesting Life
paragraphs: The symptom can symbolically ask what experience you are trying to digest that your body knows is not yours to carry.
The cramp becomes the body's clenched punctuation mark.

influenceLayers.title: Smoothing the Gut Field
paragraphs: The goal is not to force the gut to relax. The goal is to make it safe enough to unclench.
Warmth, longer exhales, gentle pressure, and naming the swallowed truth can support the reset pattern for mild, familiar cramps.

reset.title: Spleen Qi Smoothing & Enteric Downregulation
steps: Sit or lie down and place both palms over the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, imagining warmth spreading through the abdomen.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, letting the belly soften under your hands.
On each exhale, whisper: I release what I should have said. I digest what is mine. I let go of what is not.
Continue for 2 minutes. Stop if pain worsens or feels unusual.

naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a diagnosis or cure. Severe, worsening, or unusual abdominal pain needs medical evaluation.

items.label: Warmth
items.detail: A warm compress may support mild cramping or tension.

items.label: Hydration
items.detail: Small sips of water or electrolyte fluids may help when cramps are related to dehydration, diarrhea, or constipation.

items.label: Gentle movement
items.detail: Slow walking may help gas and motility for mild familiar cramps.

items.label: Food pattern tracking
items.detail: Track meals, stress, bowel changes, and timing instead of blaming one random sandwich like it acted alone.

avoid: Do not ignore severe, localized, persistent, or worsening pain.
Do not use laxatives, anti-diarrheals, or supplements without care if symptoms are severe or unusual.

brutalActions: Ask: what am I not saying?
Write the real sentence, not the polite version.
Put warmth on the belly and exhale longer than you inhale.
If pain is severe, unusual, or worsening, get medical care instead of trying to journal your appendix into compliance.

clinical.mechanism: Abdominal cramps can involve gut-brain axis activation, enteric nervous system sensitivity, irregular motility, gas pressure, constipation, diarrhea, and smooth muscle spasm.
protocol: Use warmth, hydration, and gentle movement for mild familiar cramps.
Track food, stress, bowel pattern, and timing.
Seek medical care for severe, worsening, localized, persistent, feverish, bloody, pregnancy-related, or unusual pain.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for diagnosis or medical care.

witty.metaphorText: Your intestines are a holiday airport conveyor belt run by exhausted TSA agents who keep flagging every bag as suspicious.
witty.wittyAdvice: Claim one emotional suitcase before your gut sends the entire airport into lockdown.

protocolSteps: Write it without making it polite.
Use warmth and longer exhales for mild familiar cramps.
Get medical care if pain is severe, unusual, or worsening.

hardware.hapticProfile: Sharp, irritating, stinging pulses alternating with maddening silence and a single prick.

hero.primaryPattern: Skin irritation, moisture, friction, hygiene habits, inflammation, and holding/release symbolism may overlap in pruritus ani.
hero.safetyPreview: Bleeding, pain, discharge, persistent rash, worms, infection signs, or itching that does not improve needs medical evaluation.

biologyPathway.title: Skin Irritation
biologyPathway.detail: Anal itching often starts with irritation of delicate perianal skin. Moisture, stool residue, sweat, diarrhea, friction, tight clothing, scented products, or aggressive wiping can all contribute.

biologyPathway.title: Barrier Damage
biologyPathway.short: Scratching and over-cleaning make it worse.
biologyPathway.detail: Scratching may feel relieving for approximately three glorious seconds, then damages the skin barrier and intensifies the itch-burn cycle.

biologyPathway.title: Medical Causes
biologyPathway.short: There may be a physical driver.
biologyPathway.detail: Possible contributors include hemorrhoids, fissures, yeast or fungal irritation, dermatitis, eczema, psoriasis, pinworms, infections, leakage, or bowel changes.

biologyPathway.title: Itch-Scratch Loop
biologyPathway.short: The more you scratch, the louder it gets.
biologyPathway.detail: Itching drives scratching. Scratching inflames the skin. Inflamed skin itches more. The loop is stupid, effective, and deeply committed to ruining your evening.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Local Irritation
body: Anal itching is medically about irritated skin around the anus. It can come from moisture, stool residue, sweating, diarrhea, friction, clothing, scented products, or hygiene habits.
This area is delicate. Over-cleaning can be just as irritating as under-cleaning, because apparently the body enjoys making moderation the most annoying answer.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Barrier Damage
body: Scratching, harsh soaps, scented wipes, and repeated wiping can damage the skin barrier.
Once the barrier is irritated, even normal moisture or friction can feel intense.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Possible Medical Drivers
body: Common contributors can include hemorrhoids, fissures, yeast or fungal irritation, dermatitis, eczema, psoriasis, leakage, diarrhea, constipation, pinworms, or infection.
Persistent symptoms deserve evaluation instead of heroic bathroom detective work.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Itch-Scratch Cycle
body: The itch drives scratching. Scratching causes inflammation. Inflammation creates more itching.
Breaking the loop usually means calming the skin, reducing irritants, keeping the area dry, and getting care if symptoms persist or include red flags.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary at the Exit Gate
paragraphs: Your anus is now a cranky, overworked security guard at the exit gate of a storage facility that has been at 110 percent capacity since 2013.
His name is Gary. He has not had a vacation in a decade. The gate is rusted. The hinges are screaming. There are way too many boxes behind the gate.
The gate was designed to open and close with ease. But Gary has been told to keep everything secure because you never know when you might need something you threw away.
So the gate stays tense. Boxes pile up. The hinges squeak. The area gets irritated. That is the itch. The gate is screaming: I am tired of holding all of this.
The storage facility has a sign that says CLOSED FOR INVENTORY. It has been up for years. The inventory is every grudge, grievance, old wound, and identity you keep refusing to release.
The security office has a mug that says WORLD'S OKAYEST GATEKEEPER. The gate is now a fire hazard. The itching is not the whole problem. The itching is the alarm.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Exit Gate Reality Check
paragraphs: You may not have only an anal itching problem. You may also have an I cannot let go problem. A holding on feels safer than trusting problem.
Medically, yes, check irritation, moisture, wiping, hemorrhoids, fissures, dermatitis, infection, and all the unglamorous body mechanics nobody wants printed on a motivational mug.
Symbolically, though, ask the question: what are you holding onto?
Not just the obvious grudge. The deeper thing. The identity. The story. The version of yourself that depends on being wronged.
Scratching gives a second of relief and then makes the loop worse. Very human. Very on-brand. Very similar to replaying the same resentment and wondering why you still feel inflamed.
The next time the itch demands attention, ask: what am I refusing to release?
Let go of the story. Let go of the grievance. Let go of the version of you that thinks clenching is the same thing as safety.
Open the gate safely or keep scratching at the alarm. The body does love its crude little metaphors.

influenceLayers.title: Large Intestine Release
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Large Intestine is associated with elimination and release.
Symbolically, this pattern asks what is no longer useful but still being held.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Fear & Control
paragraphs: The Kidney pattern relates to fear, willpower, survival, and deep reserves.
If release feels unsafe, the body may keep tightening around what should pass.

influenceLayers.title: The Gatekeeper Archetype
paragraphs: The Gatekeeper decides what leaves and what stays.
When the Gatekeeper is anxious, everything gets held too long.

influenceLayers.title: Shame and Release
paragraphs: This symptom can carry shame because of where it happens.
The symbolic work is to separate bodily care from embarrassment. The body is not immoral. It is irritated.

influenceLayers.title: Opening the Gate Safely
paragraphs: The goal is not dramatic surrender. The goal is safe release.
Relax the pelvic floor, reduce irritants, stop scratching, and let the body learn that letting go is allowed.

reset.title: Large Intestine Release & Pelvic Floor Softening
steps: Sit on the edge of a chair with feet flat and hands on thighs.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, letting the belly and pelvic floor stay soft.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining the pelvic floor unclenching by one percent.
On each exhale, whisper: I release what I no longer need to hold. I am safe to let go.
Continue for 2 minutes. Do not scratch during the practice. The app cannot believe this sentence is necessary, but here we are.

biologyPathway.title: Motility Slows
biologyPathway.detail: When stool stays in the colon longer than usual, more water gets reabsorbed and stool becomes harder.

biologyPathway.title: Stool Hardens
biologyPathway.detail: Low fluid, low fiber, medications, low activity, IBS-C, pelvic floor dysfunction, or other medical issues can make stool harder to pass.

biologyPathway.title: Straining Loop
biologyPathway.detail: Straining may increase pelvic floor tension and make release harder, which is a beautifully stupid design feature.

biologyPathway.title: Escalation Risk
biologyPathway.detail: Severe pain, vomiting, swelling, inability to pass gas, blood, black stool, or sudden major change can point to more serious causes.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Slow Transit
body: Constipation is medically about difficult, infrequent, incomplete, or hard bowel movements.
It can happen when intestinal transit slows and stool sits long enough for too much water to be reabsorbed.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Contributing Factors
body: Common contributors include low fiber, low fluid, low activity, ignoring the urge, medication effects, IBS-C, pelvic floor dysfunction, pregnancy, hypothyroidism, neurologic conditions, or other medical issues.
Stress can contribute by changing routine, tension, appetite, hydration, and autonomic balance.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Strain and Tension
body: The harder you strain, the more the pelvic floor may tighten.
Sometimes the exit problem is not a lack of effort. It is too much effort pointed at the wrong muscle system.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Constipation with severe pain, vomiting, belly swelling, inability to pass gas, blood, black stool, unexplained weight loss, or sudden major change deserves medical care.
The colon is not a place to practice denial as a wellness modality.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Recycling Center Shutdown
paragraphs: Your colon is now a chronically understaffed recycling center that has not had a pickup in weeks.
The conveyor belt is jammed. The workers are on strike. Management keeps sending memos that say WE NEED MORE PRODUCT while the product piles up and starts to smell.
Cortisol and Adrenaline keep hitting the STOP button because you never know when you might need that old yogurt container.
The workers hung a sign on the break room door that says WE WON'T MOVE UNTIL YOU RELAX.
Management responds by calling a three-hour meeting about why nothing is moving. The meeting achieves nothing, because of course it does.
There is a poster on the wall that says TEAMWORK MAKES THE BOWEL MOVEMENT. It is crooked. Nobody has the energy to fix it.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Holding-On Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a constipation problem. You may also have an I cannot let go problem.
What are you holding onto? Not just food. Not just waste. The story, the control, the grievance, the old identity, the version of you that thinks release equals danger.
You push. Nothing. You push harder. Still nothing. Very symbolic. Deeply irritating.
Fiber can help. Water can help. Walking can help. Medical care can help. But if your whole life is clench, control, resist, repeat, your body may learn the choreography.
The next useful question is not how hard can I force this. It is what needs to soften so movement can happen.
Release is not collapse. Letting go is not losing everything. Sometimes the exit is just an exit.
Open the exit gently or keep living like a storage unit with organs. Elegant? No. Accurate? Unfortunately.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Transformation
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Spleen is associated with processing and transforming.
When worry dominates, the symbolic processing system can feel slow, heavy, and overloaded.

influenceLayers.title: Large Intestine Release
paragraphs: The Large Intestine is associated with elimination and letting go.
Constipation symbolically asks what is still being held after it has stopped being useful.

influenceLayers.title: The Hoarder's Gut
paragraphs: The Releaser is the part that knows how to be done.
When that part is suppressed, the gut becomes a storage room instead of a river.

influenceLayers.title: Control Pattern
paragraphs: This symptom can mirror the belief that if you release, you will lose control.
The body may be asking for trust instead of more force.

influenceLayers.title: Softening the Exit
paragraphs: The goal is not aggressive pushing. The goal is coordinated release.
Breath, hydration, fiber as tolerated, movement, routine, and pelvic floor softening can support that process.

steps: Sit on the toilet or edge of a chair with feet supported. A small footstool can help create a squat-like posture.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds and let the belly expand.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds and relax the jaw, belly, and pelvic floor.
On each exhale, whisper: I release what I no longer need to hold. I am safe to let go.
Do not strain. If nothing happens, pause and try again later.

Walk gently after fiber instead of waiting for magic.
Stop straining and soften the pelvic floor.
Get care for severe pain, vomiting, swelling, blood, black stool, inability to pass gas, or persistent constipation.

biologyPathway.title: Transit Speeds Up
biologyPathway.detail: Diarrhea can happen when stool moves through the intestines too quickly for water to be absorbed properly.

biologyPathway.title: Fluid Stays in the Stool
biologyPathway.detail: Infections, irritation, inflammation, certain foods, medications, anxiety, and stress can all alter fluid handling in the gut.

biologyPathway.title: Enteric Alarm
biologyPathway.short: The gut's nerve network gets reactive.
biologyPathway.detail: The enteric nervous system coordinates motility and secretion. When irritated or stressed, it may push contents through with rude, unnecessary urgency.

biologyPathway.title: Dehydration Risk
biologyPathway.short: Fluid loss can become the danger.
biologyPathway.detail: The symptom may be embarrassing, but the real risk is dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, or missing a more serious cause.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Rapid Transit
body: Diarrhea is medically a digestive symptom. It can happen when stool moves through the intestines too quickly or when excess fluid remains in the stool.

Possible causes include viral or bacterial infection, food poisoning, food intolerance, medication effects, stress, anxiety, IBS, inflammatory bowel disease, gallbladder issues, or other medical causes.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Enteric Nervous System Activation
body: The gut has its own nerve network, the enteric nervous system. It helps regulate motility, secretion, and sensitivity.
Stress does not make diarrhea fake. Stress can change gut movement, urgency, sensitivity, and how quickly the system tries to clear itself.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Water Absorption Failure
body: When transit is too fast, the colon has less time to absorb water.
The result can be loose stool, urgency, cramping, bloating, and that deeply undignified sprint toward porcelain civilization.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: The biggest concern is dehydration or a serious underlying cause.

Blood, black stool, fever, severe abdominal pain, repeated vomiting, signs of dehydration, diarrhea after antibiotics, recent travel, pregnancy, immunocompromised status, or symptoms lasting more than a couple days should be taken seriously.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Firefighter With Too Many False Alarms
paragraphs: Your colon is now a chronically overworked, underpaid firefighter who has been responding to false alarms for years.
The siren is always going off. The hose is always leaking. The fire is either a real digestive issue or your own panic wearing a tiny firefighter helmet.
Normally, the firefighter responds with appropriate urgency. But stress hormones named Cortisol and Adrenaline keep calling in every emotion as a five-alarm fire.
A thought? Emergency. A deadline? Emergency. A suspicious taco? Emergency with sirens.
The firefighter stops inspecting the scene and just blasts everything out. That is diarrhea: evacuate first, process later.

The fire station poster says EVACUATE FIRST. PROCESS LATER. Someone wrote STOP SENDING FALSE ALARMS underneath. Nobody listened, because apparently the management committee is made of caffeine and unresolved dread.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Overwhelm Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a diarrhea problem. You may also have an everything is urgent problem.
Your body may be saying: I cannot hold this, process this, contain this, and pretend I am fine at the same time.
You have been pushing through, handling everything, absorbing chaos, and calling it normal because apparently humans think suffering quietly is a personality trait.
Food, infection, medication, inflammation, and medical issues absolutely matter. Do not turn every bowel movement into a childhood analysis seminar.
But if diarrhea shows up every time life becomes too much, ask the useful question: what am I trying to evacuate before I have actually processed it?
You may need fluids. You may need rest. You may need medical care. You may also need to stop living like every inconvenience is a burning building.
Ask for help before your body starts filing emergency reports through your colon.
Lower the emergency setting or keep evacuating every signal like a disaster drill.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Containment
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Spleen is associated with processing, transforming, and containing.
Symbolically, diarrhea can mirror a system that cannot hold long enough to process.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Fear & Collapse
paragraphs: The Kidney pattern relates to fear, reserves, and the deep sense of safety.
When safety feels gone, the symbolic body may stop containing and start evacuating.

influenceLayers.title: The Leaky Vessel Archetype
paragraphs: The Container is the part that can hold experience without cracking.
When overwhelmed, the vessel leaks. The body says: this is too much to hold.

influenceLayers.title: Urgency Pattern
paragraphs: This symptom can ask where life has become all urgency and no digestion.
The body may be trying to clear something before it knows what it is.

influenceLayers.title: Containment Reset
paragraphs: The symbolic goal is not clenching harder. It is safe containment.
Breath, warmth, fluids, and support tell the body it does not have to evacuate every signal immediately.

reset.title: Spleen Qi Containing & Kidney Anchoring
steps: Sit or lie down and place both hands on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, imagining warmth spreading through the abdomen.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining heaviness draining down through the feet.
On each exhale, whisper: I am safe. I can hold what is mine. I release what I do not need to hold.
Continue for 2 minutes. Prioritize fluids and medical care if red flags are present.

items.detail: Simple tolerated foods may help while symptoms settle.

items.detail: Track meals, travel, sick contacts, medications, stress, and timing.

Ask: what feels too urgent to process?
Lower demand on your system for a few hours.
Get care for dehydration, blood, fever, severe pain, recent antibiotics, travel illness, pregnancy, or persistent symptoms.

Seek care for dehydration, blood, fever, severe pain, persistent symptoms, recent antibiotics, pregnancy, or high-risk health status.

biologyPathway.title: Gas Formation
biologyPathway.detail: Gas can come from swallowed air, carbonation, and fermentation of carbohydrates by gut bacteria.

biologyPathway.title: Motility and Trapping
biologyPathway.detail: If motility is slow, irregular, or blocked by constipation, gas can become trapped and stretch the intestinal wall.

biologyPathway.title: Stretch Pain
biologyPathway.detail: Trapped gas stretches bowel tissue and activates mechanoreceptors, creating sharp, shifting, crampy, or stabbing pain.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger Pattern
biologyPathway.detail: Food intolerance, IBS, constipation, stress tension, eating quickly, chewing gum, and carbonated drinks can all contribute.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Gas Production
body: Gas pain and flatulence are medically digestive symptoms. Gas can come from swallowed air, carbonation, and bacterial fermentation of food.
Common contributors include beans, dairy intolerance, high-FODMAP foods, constipation, eating quickly, chewing gum, IBS, and changes in gut motility.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Trapped Pressure
body: Gas becomes painful when it gets trapped or moves poorly through the intestines.
The bowel wall stretches, pressure builds, and pain signals fire because the gut is a dramatic tube with nerve endings. Wonderful engineering.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Stress and Tension
body: Stress can change breathing, diaphragm tension, abdominal bracing, motility, and gut sensitivity.
That does not mean gas is imaginary. It means the nervous system can make pressure feel louder.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Mild familiar gas is different from severe or persistent abdominal symptoms.
Severe pain, persistent bloating, vomiting, fever, blood, black stool, chest pain, weight loss, or inability to pass gas or stool should be evaluated.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Broken Elevator Apartment Building
paragraphs: Your gut is now an overstuffed apartment building with one broken elevator.
The tenants are gas bubbles. They are trapped in hallways, stairwells, and air ducts, complaining to management with the confidence of people who have never paid rent.
The landlord, your stress hormones, keeps saying EVERYTHING IS FINE while the building groans like a haunted duplex.
The maintenance guy, your parasympathetic nervous system, has not been seen since 2017. He left a sticky note that says GONE FISHING.
A burp or fart releases one tenant. Great. But if the elevator is still broken, the pressure builds again.
The goal is not to shame the tenants. The goal is to fix movement through the building before the plumbing files a formal complaint.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Pressure Release Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a gas problem. You may also have a pressure management problem.
Maybe it is food. Maybe it is dairy, beans, carbonation, constipation, IBS, eating too fast, or your gut bacteria hosting a tiny music festival.
But if gas pain shows up every time you swallow irritation, avoid conflict, and call pressure peace, ask the useful question: what am I not releasing?
The pressure does not disappear because you act calm. It moves somewhere.
Sometimes it comes out as tension. Sometimes it comes out as bloating. Sometimes your body chooses comedy and makes it everyone else's problem.
Say what you mean safely. Move your body. Breathe lower. Stop letting every unspoken sentence become a trapped tenant in your abdomen.
Let the pressure out or keep living like a shaken soda bottle. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Qi Stagnation
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Liver is associated with smooth flow.
Symbolically, trapped gas can mirror blocked movement, stuck irritation, or pressure that has no clean outlet.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Transformation
paragraphs: The Spleen pattern is associated with processing and transforming.
When worry and overthinking dominate, digestion can symbolically feel bogged down and gassy.

influenceLayers.title: Pressure Cooker Archetype
paragraphs: The Expresser releases pressure before it becomes explosive.
When suppressed, the gut becomes the pressure chamber and gas becomes the rude little messenger.

influenceLayers.title: Trapped Voice
paragraphs: This symptom can ask where irritation is being swallowed instead of expressed.
The body may be looking for a safer exit.

influenceLayers.title: Moving the Elevator
paragraphs: The symbolic reset is movement without force.
Walking, breath, warmth, and honest naming can tell the system: pressure is allowed to move.

reset.title: Liver Qi Smoothing & Gut Pressure Release
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, or sit comfortably.
Place both hands on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds and let the belly expand gently.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining pressure moving downward and outward.
On each exhale, whisper: I release pressure safely. I let what is trapped move.

naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Severe, persistent, or unusual gas pain should be medically evaluated.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Reflux Pressure
body: Heartburn is medically a reflux symptom. It usually happens when stomach contents move upward into the esophagus.
Meal size, late eating, lying down after meals, abdominal pressure, trigger foods, smoking, alcohol, medications, pregnancy, and hiatal hernia can contribute.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Lower Esophageal Sphincter Function
body: The lower esophageal sphincter is the valve-like barrier between the stomach and esophagus.
When it relaxes inappropriately, weakens, or is overwhelmed by pressure, reflux can happen.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Esophageal Irritation
body: The esophagus is not designed for repeated acid exposure.
Symptoms can include burning, sour taste, regurgitation, cough, hoarseness, nausea, throat irritation, or chest discomfort.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Chest pain should not automatically be assumed to be heartburn.
Trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, unexplained weight loss, persistent vomiting, severe symptoms, or ongoing reflux should be medically evaluated.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary and the Backwards Sewer System
paragraphs: Your stomach is a sewer tank with a top valve that is supposed to stay closed.
Gary the pump operator has not had a break since 2004. Under pressure, Gary leaves the valve cracked open.
The acid backs up into the esophagus, which is absolutely not built for sewer-water tourism.
The control room has a DON'T PANIC sign in Comic Sans and a coffee mug that says WORLD'S OKAYEST PUMP OPERATOR.
Antacids may lower the burn, but they do not always fix the pressure, timing, valve behavior, or the habit of eating like consequences were invented by cowards.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Swallowed Fire Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a reflux problem. You may also have an I will just keep it down problem.
Food matters. Meal timing matters. Medication guidance matters. Chest pain red flags matter. Do not turn GERD into a poetry slam and ignore medicine.
But if the burn rises every time you swallow anger and perform peace, ask what truth keeps climbing back up.
Your throat does not need to become a chimney for every sentence you were too tired, scared, or trained to say.
Cool the fire. Speak safely. Stay upright. Track triggers. Get care when symptoms cross the line.
Cool the fire or keep feeding the chimney. Your choice.

brutalActions: Stay upright after meals.

biologyPathway.title: Pressure Builds
biologyPathway.detail: Straining, constipation, diarrhea, pregnancy, prolonged sitting, and heavy lifting can increase pressure around hemorrhoidal vessels.

biologyPathway.title: Veins and Tissue Swell
biologyPathway.detail: Hemorrhoids involve swollen vascular tissue around the anus or lower rectum.

biologyPathway.title: Irritation and Bleeding
biologyPathway.short: Friction can cause pain or blood.
biologyPathway.detail: Passing stool, wiping, or ongoing pressure can irritate swollen tissue and sometimes cause bright red bleeding.

biologyPathway.title: Strain-Clench Loop
biologyPathway.detail: Pain may cause clenching, and clenching may worsen difficulty releasing. The loop is not bright, but it is committed.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Pressure and Straining
body: Hemorrhoids are medically swollen blood vessels/tissue cushions around the anus or lower rectum.
Common contributors include constipation, straining, diarrhea, pregnancy, prolonged sitting, heavy lifting, aging, and increased abdominal or pelvic pressure.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Swelling and Irritation
body: The swollen tissue can itch, burn, ache, sting, bleed bright red, or feel like a lump.
Pain can make a person clench, strain, or avoid bowel movements, which can worsen the cycle.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Soft Exit Strategy
body: The goal is easier stool, less straining, less irritation, and less pressure.
Fluids, fiber as tolerated, movement, footstool posture, warm sitz baths, and avoiding long toilet sessions may help mild cases.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Heavy bleeding, black stool, severe pain, fever, pus, rapidly worsening swelling, or persistent bleeding needs medical evaluation.
Do not assume all rectal bleeding is hemorrhoids. The body is annoying, not always obvious.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary the Nightclub Bouncer
paragraphs: Your anus is now a nightclub with a cranky bouncer named Gary.
The velvet rope is swollen, the line is out the door, and Gary has been told to keep everyone secure even though half the crowd should have left hours ago.
Every time you strain, Gary tries to hold the door closed while the entire crowd pushes forward.
The hinges scream. The rope stretches. The fire marshal, also known as your body, starts sending notices.
The hemorrhoid is not the villain. It is the swollen evidence that the door has been forced too many times.
Gary does not need a pep talk. Gary needs less pressure, softer exits, and maybe for you to stop doomscrolling on the toilet like it is a command center.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Forced Exit Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a hemorrhoid problem. You may also have a forcing-the-exit problem.
Stop treating bowel movements like a competitive event. Straining harder is not strength. It is how you turn a bathroom trip into a vascular protest.
Medically, soften the stool, reduce pressure, stop sitting forever, and get bleeding or severe pain checked.
Symbolically, ask where you are pushing against a door that needs softening, not violence.
You do not need more force. You need timing, hydration, patience, movement, and a body that trusts release.
Stop forcing the door or keep paying interest in swelling. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Large Intestine Release

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Reserve and Fear

influenceLayers.title: Clenched Gate Archetype

When suppressed, release becomes a fight and the gate takes the damage.

The body may be asking for less force and more trust.

influenceLayers.title: Soft Exit
paragraphs: The symbolic reset is softening instead of pushing.
Breath, foot support, hydration, fiber as tolerated, and shorter toilet sessions can support that pattern.

reset.title: Pelvic Floor Softening & Pressure Release
steps: Sit on the edge of a chair or toilet with feet supported.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds and relax the jaw and belly.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining the pelvic floor softening by one percent.
On each exhale, whisper: I release without force. I do not have to push against a closed door.
Continue for 2 minutes. Do not strain.

biologyPathway.title: Wall Weakness
biologyPathway.detail: A hernia happens when tissue pushes through a weak area in muscle or connective tissue. This can be congenital, age-related, surgical, pressure-related, or strain-related.

biologyPathway.title: Pressure Rises
biologyPathway.detail: Heavy lifting, coughing, constipation, pregnancy, obesity, repeated straining, and physical overuse can increase pressure against the wall.

biologyPathway.title: Bulge or Pain
biologyPathway.detail: The result may be a visible or felt bulge, aching, pressure, burning, pulling, or pain that worsens with strain.

biologyPathway.title: Trapping Risk
biologyPathway.detail: If tissue becomes stuck or blood supply is threatened, the situation can become serious. Severe pain, vomiting, redness, fever, or a trapped tender bulge needs urgent care.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Structural Weak Point
body: A hernia is medically a structural tissue-wall problem.
It occurs when tissue or part of an organ pushes through a weak spot in muscle or connective tissue.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Pressure and Strain
body: Common contributors can include heavy lifting, chronic coughing, constipation/straining, pregnancy, prior surgery, aging, obesity, or congenital tissue weakness.
Stress may contribute indirectly if it leads to overwork, bracing, poor recovery, or repeated strain, but it is not the sole cause.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Bulge and Discomfort
body: A hernia may cause a bulge, pressure, aching, burning, pulling, or pain that changes with position or strain.
Some hernias are reducible. Some are not. The body did not ask for this little wall-demolition project.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Urgent Warning Signs
body: Severe pain, sudden worsening, vomiting, fever, redness, a tender bulge, a bulge that cannot be pushed back in, or inability to pass stool/gas can signal incarceration or strangulation.
Those symptoms need urgent medical care, not a breathing exercise and a brave little speech.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overstuffed Storage Unit
paragraphs: Your abdominal wall is now a storage unit that has been at 150 percent capacity since 2008.
The walls are bulging. The door will not close. Management keeps saying just one more box while the building groans like an exhausted grandparent.
Every I'll handle it is another box. Every don't worry about me is another box. Every I can take more is a refrigerator shoved into a unit that was built for patio furniture.
The building inspector, also known as your body, has been leaving notes: WALL AT RISK. REMOVE WEIGHT.
Then a box finds a weak spot and pushes through. That is the hernia. The wall is not being dramatic. The wall has receipts.
The sign in the office says WE'RE FINE. Someone wrote WE'RE NOT underneath in fresh red marker. The marker is correct.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Over-Functioner Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a hernia problem. You may also have an I'll handle it problem.
How many times have you carried more than your body, schedule, or soul could reasonably hold?
The hernia is not a character weakness. It is a wall problem. And walls fail when pressure keeps rising.
Medical care matters. Lifting restrictions matter. Urgent warning signs matter. Do not try to mindset your way through a trapped hernia like a motivational idiot.
Symbolically, though, the message is brutally simple: put something down.
You are not Atlas. The sky was never yours to carry. Delegating is not weakness. Rest is not failure. Asking for help is not collapse.
Put the boxes down or keep letting the wall pay for your pride. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Qi Lifting
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Spleen is associated with lifting and containing.
Symbolically, hernia patterns can mirror a support system that has been asked to hold too much for too long.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Reserve

When reserves feel depleted, the symbolic wall may feel unsupported.

influenceLayers.title: Collapsed Boundary Archetype

When suppressed, the person may over-function until the body becomes the boundary enforcer.

influenceLayers.title: Atlas Pattern

The wall weakens when every no becomes a yes.

influenceLayers.title: Unload the Unit

The medical reset is evaluation, avoiding strain, and following clinician guidance.

reset.title: Boundary Unloading & Pressure Downshift
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, unless that position worsens symptoms.
Place hands gently on the lower belly, not pressing into the bulge.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds and imagine setting down one box.
Whisper: I do not have to hold everything. I am allowed to ask for help.

biologyPathway.title: Urine Concentration
biologyPathway.detail: When urine becomes concentrated, minerals and salts are more likely to crystallize.

biologyPathway.title: Stone Formation
biologyPathway.detail: Stone type matters. Calcium oxalate, uric acid, struvite, cystine, and other stones can have different causes and prevention strategies.

biologyPathway.title: Stone Movement
biologyPathway.short: Pain spikes when the stone moves.
biologyPathway.detail: A stone moving through the urinary tract can cause severe flank pain, waves of pain, nausea, vomiting, blood in urine, and urinary urgency.

biologyPathway.title: Infection or Blockage Risk
biologyPathway.detail: Fever, chills, inability to urinate, uncontrolled pain, or vomiting can signal complications that need urgent care.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Concentrated Urine
body: Kidney stones form when minerals and salts crystallize in urine.
Dehydration, low urine volume, diet patterns, genetics, medications, metabolic factors, infection, and urinary tract conditions can all contribute.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Stone Type Matters
body: Not all kidney stones are the same.
Different stone types can require different prevention strategies, which is why actual evaluation matters more than guessing and drinking mystery wellness sludge.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Renal Colic
body: When a stone moves into the ureter, it can cause severe waves of flank or abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, urinary urgency, and blood in the urine.
The pain can be intense because the ureter is narrow and deeply uninterested in hosting jagged mineral souvenirs.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Urgent Warning Signs
body: Fever, chills, uncontrolled pain, repeated vomiting, inability to urinate, severe weakness, or signs of infection need urgent care.
An infected obstructing stone can be dangerous. Do not attempt to breathe through that like a monk with bad judgment.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Water Treatment Plant Turning Into Gravel
paragraphs: Your kidneys are a water-treatment plant running on emergency drought protocol.
The filters are clogged. The pipes are scaling. The supervisor keeps yelling CONSERVE EVERYTHING while the plant slowly turns into a beige mineral dungeon.
Normally, water flows, waste leaves, minerals stay dissolved, and everyone clocks out by five.
But when the flow gets low and the chemistry gets rude, minerals start gathering like bored construction workers.
Eventually, they form a stone. A tiny jagged landlord of pain.
The plant has a DON'T PANIC sign in Comic Sans. Someone wrote TOO LATE underneath. Honestly, fair.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Hardened Fear Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a kidney stone problem. You may also have a control problem.
Drink water. Get evaluated. Know your stone type. Follow actual medical guidance. Do not pretend fear release replaces urinalysis, imaging, or pain control.
Symbolically, though, ask what you have been hardening around.
How many disasters have you rehearsed? How many what-ifs have you polished into little internal rocks?
Catastrophizing is not preparation. It is a gravel machine with anxiety branding.
Let the river move. Stop bracing for every future impact like your worry is a security system.
Let the river move or keep turning fear into gravel. Your choice.

steps: Sit upright or lie comfortably if symptoms allow.

Whisper: I release the fear I have hardened around. I allow flow.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger Signal
biologyPathway.detail: Nausea can begin from the gut, brain, inner ear, hormones, medications, pain, toxins, infection, pregnancy, migraine, or stress.

biologyPathway.title: Brain-Gut Alarm
biologyPathway.detail: The brain coordinates nausea and vomiting signals through the nervous system, gut, inner ear, and chemical sensors.

biologyPathway.title: Gastric Disruption
biologyPathway.detail: The stomach may slow, churn irregularly, or prepare to reject contents. This can feel like waves, queasiness, salivation, sweating, and loss of appetite.

biologyPathway.title: Red Flag Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Chest pain, severe abdominal pain, dehydration, neurologic symptoms, poisoning, pregnancy concerns, severe headache, or persistent vomiting changes the safety level.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Nausea Is a Warning Symptom
body: Nausea is not one disease. It is a warning signal with many possible causes.

Common possibilities include stomach infection, food poisoning, pregnancy, medication effects, migraine, motion sickness, reflux, gallbladder problems, dehydration, anxiety, pain, toxins, or other illness.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Brain-Gut Coordination
body: The brain, gut, inner ear, hormones, and chemical sensors can all contribute to nausea.
This is why nausea can come from a stomach bug, a migraine, a boat ride, a panic spike, or one cursed burrito with legal immunity.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Rejection Response
body: The body may slow stomach emptying, increase salivation, trigger sweating, reduce appetite, and prepare for vomiting.
Symbolically, nausea can feel like the body saying: I cannot keep this down.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Seek medical care for chest pain, severe abdominal pain, dehydration, vomiting blood, black stool, severe headache, neurologic symptoms, fever with stiff neck, pregnancy concerns, poisoning risk, or persistent vomiting.
Nausea is allowed to be emotional. It is not allowed to skip the medical red-flag checklist like a shady wellness influencer.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary the Dishwasher and the Rotten Plates
paragraphs: Your stomach is an overworked dishwasher named Gary.
Normally, Gary handles plates efficiently: soak, scrub, rinse, repeat. A humble little digestive employee.

But the restaurant manager keeps sending down rotten plates: stress, bad food, medication side effects, motion sickness, migraine signals, pregnancy hormones, and conversations you are still replaying in your head.
The sink is overflowing. The smell is suspicious. Gary has that look that says he may walk out or lose his lunch.
The health inspector, your body, keeps leaving notes: DISHWASHER OVERWORKED. FOOD QUESTIONABLE. CLOSE IMMEDIATELY.
The nausea is Gary's resignation letter. It says: I cannot scrub one more toxic plate.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Rejection Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a nausea problem. You may also have an I cannot keep swallowing this pattern.

Check the medical stuff first. Pregnancy risk, infection, medications, migraine, dehydration, food poisoning, gallbladder, reflux, heart symptoms, and toxins do not care about your emotional metaphors.
Symbolically, though, nausea is often the body saying: I cannot stomach this.
What are you forcing yourself to accept? What situation makes your body recoil before your mouth can explain why?
Not everything deserves to be digested. Some things are meant to be rejected.
Sip fluids if safe. Rest. Check red flags. Then name the thing you keep swallowing.
Spit out the poison or keep letting Gary scrub rotten plates. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Stomach Qi Rebellion
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, Stomach Qi normally descends.
Symbolically, nausea can mirror a reversal: what should move down rises back up.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Qi Invading Stomach
paragraphs: The Liver pattern is associated with frustration and smooth flow.
When flow is blocked, the symbolic stomach may rebel instead of process.

influenceLayers.title: Rejected Truth Archetype
paragraphs: The Spitter knows how to reject what is toxic.
When that archetype is suppressed, the stomach may carry the refusal.

influenceLayers.title: Disgust Signal
paragraphs: Nausea can symbolically ask what your body finds unacceptable.
The symptom may be a no before the mind has permission to say no.

influenceLayers.title: Descending and Rejecting
paragraphs: The goal is not to force everything down.
The goal is to calm the body and clearly reject what does not belong.

reset.title: Stomach Qi Descending & Safe Rejection
steps: Sit upright or lie on your side if that feels better.
Place one hand on the upper stomach and one hand on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining the stomach settling downward.
Whisper: I do not have to swallow what does not serve me. I can reject what is toxic.

biologyPathway.title: Exposure
biologyPathway.detail: Tapeworm infection can occur after ingesting eggs or larvae, often through contaminated food, undercooked meat or fish, or sanitation-related exposure.

biologyPathway.title: Attachment and Growth
biologyPathway.short: The worm can live in the intestine.
biologyPathway.detail: Some tapeworms attach to the intestinal lining and absorb nutrients. This is not a metaphorical roommate situation. It is a parasite.

biologyPathway.title: Symptoms or Segments
biologyPathway.short: Signs may be obvious or subtle.
biologyPathway.detail: Symptoms can include abdominal discomfort, nausea, diarrhea, appetite changes, weight loss, weakness, or visible segments in stool. Some infections can be mild or unnoticed.

biologyPathway.title: Complication Check
biologyPathway.short: Some parasite infections can become serious.
biologyPathway.detail: Depending on species and exposure, complications can involve blocked ducts, nutritional problems, or neurologic symptoms. Evaluation matters because worm vibes are not a diagnostic category.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Parasite Exposure
body: Tapeworm is medically an intestinal parasite, not an emotional state with teeth.
Possible exposure can include contaminated food, undercooked meat or fish, ingesting eggs or larvae, travel, sanitation issues, or contact with infected sources.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Intestinal Attachment
body: Some tapeworms attach to the intestine and absorb nutrients from the host.
Symptoms may include abdominal discomfort, nausea, diarrhea, appetite changes, weight loss, weakness, or visible segments in stool.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Diagnosis and Treatment
body: Tapeworm infection should be evaluated medically with appropriate testing.
Treatment usually involves antiparasitic medication prescribed by a clinician. The app is not going to recommend backyard worm warfare, because apparently we still value civilization.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Seek medical care for visible worm segments, unexplained weight loss, persistent abdominal symptoms, vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, fever, or suspected parasite exposure.
Neurologic symptoms, seizures, severe headache, confusion, or eye symptoms need urgent evaluation.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Freeloader in Unit 4B
paragraphs: Your intestines are an apartment building, and someone moved into unit 4B without signing a lease.
This tenant eats your groceries, absorbs your nutrients, leaves you the scraps, and somehow acts like the building owes him cable.
The landlord, unfortunately, is you. You were busy managing everyone else's crisis and forgot to check who was living rent-free in the gut complex.
The lobby sign says WE'RE DOING OUR BEST. Someone wrote THEN EVICT THE WORM underneath. Honestly, fair feedback.
The eviction notice has been on the fridge for years. Today, ideally, it becomes medical testing and treatment instead of another inspirational sticky note.
Unit 4B does not need compassion. Unit 4B needs a clinician-approved eviction.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Boundary Drain Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a parasite problem. You may also have a boundary-drain pattern.
The actual worm needs actual medical treatment. Do not confuse symbolism with treatment unless your goal is to become a cautionary tale with stool samples.
Symbolically, though, ask who has been feeding on you.
What job, relationship, family role, habit, or belief keeps eating your energy and leaving you with scraps?
Your resources are not public property. Your time is not an all-you-can-eat buffet. Your body is not a hotel for freeloaders, literal or emotional.
Get the parasite treated. Then look at the pattern that made the metaphor hit a little too hard.
Evict the freeloader or keep donating your life force to unit 4B. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen-Stomach Depletion

influenceLayers.title: Boundary Breach

This layer asks where the system stopped screening what gets in and what gets to stay.

influenceLayers.title: The Parasite Archetype

It asks where your life has normalized being consumed.

influenceLayers.title: Self-Protector

It restores ownership over time, body, attention, food, money, and emotional labor.

Whisper: My energy is my own. I am not food for others. I protect my nourishment.
Then take one real-world action: schedule care, test, treat, or set one boundary.

biologyPathway.title: Protective Lining Weakens
biologyPathway.short: The stomach or duodenal lining loses protection.
biologyPathway.detail: The stomach and duodenum have protective mucus and blood flow. H. pylori, NSAIDs, smoking, alcohol, and other factors can weaken this protection.

biologyPathway.title: Acid Irritates Tissue
biologyPathway.detail: Acid helps digest food, but when protective barriers fail, acid can irritate and erode tissue.

biologyPathway.title: Ulcer Forms
biologyPathway.detail: A peptic ulcer is a sore in the stomach or upper small intestine lining. It can cause burning, gnawing pain, nausea, bloating, or appetite changes.

biologyPathway.title: Bleeding or Perforation Risk
biologyPathway.detail: Vomiting blood, black stool, sudden severe pain, fainting, severe weakness, or shock symptoms can signal bleeding or perforation.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Medical Causes First
body: Peptic ulcers are medically real sores in the stomach or upper small intestine lining.
Common causes include H. pylori infection and NSAID use. Smoking, alcohol, severe physiologic stress, and other conditions may also contribute.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Barrier Failure
body: The stomach is built to handle acid, but only when the protective lining is working.
When that lining is weakened, acid can irritate and erode tissue, creating gnawing, burning, or stabbing pain.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Testing and Treatment
body: Ulcers often need medical evaluation, especially to check for H. pylori, medication causes, bleeding risk, and proper treatment.
Breathing exercises do not kill H. pylori. I know, devastating news for the scented-candle economy.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Signs
body: Vomiting blood, coffee-ground vomit, black/tarry stool, sudden severe abdominal pain, fainting, weakness, or shock symptoms need urgent care.
Those are not journal prompts. Those are get-help-now signs.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: Gary at the Acid Plant
paragraphs: Your stomach is now a chemical plant with an acid leak.
Gary the janitor has been patching the same pipe for years with duct tape, denial, and a mug that says WORLD'S OKAYEST LINING.
The supervisors keep opening the acid valve while Gary screams that the pipe is paper-thin.
The building inspector, your body, keeps leaving notes: PIPE CORRODED. ACID LEAKING. SHUT DOWN AND CALL SOMEONE QUALIFIED.
Medication may reduce acid. Antibiotics may be needed if H. pylori is involved. Avoiding NSAIDs may matter. Gary cannot fix this with inspirational breathing alone.
The plant needs actual repair, not another staff meeting about resilience.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Acid Plant Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have an ulcer problem. You may also have a swallowing-acid pattern.
Medical first: check H. pylori, NSAID use, bleeding signs, and treatment. Do not spiritualize a hole in your digestive lining while ignoring black stool.
Symbolically, though, ask what keeps eating at you.
What resentment do you keep swallowing? What boundary keeps getting burned through? What situation digests you while you pretend you are digesting it?
Medication can lower acid. Treatment can heal tissue. Boundaries can stop you from walking back into the same acid plant every day.
Protect your lining. Protect your time. Protect your no.
Stop feeding the acid plant or let Gary keep patching holes in a burning pipe. Your choice.

The colon is not supposed to send crimson newsletters.

Colonoscopy is not glamorous, but neither is ignoring preventable disaster.

Plans depend on location and spread.

paragraphs: The waste line is backed up and one section is throwing warning flags like a sewer with a legal department.
The useful plan is not denial, not panic, and not internet supplement roulette.
The useful plan is evaluation, diagnosis, staging, treatment, support, and asking very specific questions until the medical fog starts behaving.
The symbolism can help with meaning. It does not get to fire the oncologist.

reset.title: Waste Line & Letting-Go Reset
steps: Sit somewhere safe.
Place one hand on the heart and one on the affected area if comfortable.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale slowly for 8 seconds.
Think: I am not alone. I take the next medical step. I support my body without blaming it.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Colon Cancer may require medical evaluation, diagnosis, and condition-specific treatment.

items.label: Screening
items.detail: Follow age/risk-based colorectal screening guidance.

items.label: Symptom evaluation
items.detail: Blood, anemia, bowel changes, or weight loss need medical review.

items.label: Colonoscopy/biopsy
items.detail: Diagnosis often requires visualization and tissue confirmation.

items.label: Treatment planning
items.detail: Ask about stage, tumor markers, surgery, chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and surveillance.

avoid: Do not ignore rectal bleeding or persistent bowel habit changes.
Do not assume hemorrhoids explain all bleeding without medical review.
Do not replace colonoscopy or treatment with cleanses. The irony would be unbearable.

brutalActions: Schedule or keep medical evaluation.
Write down symptoms and questions.
Bring support to appointments.
Do not let fear, shame, or miracle-cure marketing run the treatment plan.

clinical.mechanism: Colon cancer is cancer of the large intestine and overlaps with colorectal cancer screening and diagnosis. Warning signs can include blood in stool, persistent bowel habit changes, unexplained weight loss, abdominal pain, anemia, fatigue, narrow stools, or positive stool testing. Screening commonly begins at age 45 for average-risk adults, earlier with certain risks.
protocol: Track symptoms, screening history, family history, diagnosis stage if known, treatment plan, side effects, weight loss, pain, bleeding, fatigue, fever, infection signs, and urgent changes. Follow oncology guidance.
Use medical evaluation when symptoms are new, severe, progressive, persistent, recurrent, or paired with red flags.
Symbolic interpretation never replaces diagnosis, screening, emergency care, or treatment.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical care.

witty.metaphorText: The waste line is backed up and one section is throwing warning flags like a sewer with a legal department.
witty.wittyAdvice: Scope the waste line and stop pretending blood in stool is a philosophical suggestion. Your choice.

brutal.realityCheck: You may have Colon Cancer or a cancer concern. You may also have a shock-and-meaning pattern.

critical_alerts: Blood in stool, black/tarry stool, persistent rectal bleeding, or positive stool test
Persistent diarrhea/constipation/change in stool caliber, unexplained weight loss, anemia, fatigue
Severe abdominal pain, vomiting, bowel obstruction symptoms, or inability to pass stool/gas
Fever/infection signs during treatment or severe treatment side effects
seek_immediate_care_if: Severe symptoms, bleeding, trouble breathing, neurologic deficits, severe pain, infection signs during treatment, or feeling medically unsafe occur
schedule_care_if: Screening is due, stool test is positive, bowel habits change persistently, or bleeding/anemia occurs
Family history/genetic risk requires earlier or more frequent screening
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Supportive routines, sleep, nutrition, movement as tolerated, counseling, and support groups may help alongside medical care.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Cancer symptoms, screening abnormalities, new lumps, unexplained weight loss, bleeding, persistent cough, neurologic symptoms, or treatment side effects need medical guidance.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure cancer.
